programmes.show_description: It's induction day for 26 junior doctors as they pick up their passes and scrubs for the Emergency Department and try to get their bearings around the Royal Berkshire Hospital.
Junior doctor Alex handles a paracetamol overdose and a high-risk heart patient, Adnan braves his first shift in Resus, and Claire deals with a woman in agonising stomach pain.

programmes.show_name: The Gentle Art of Swedish Death Cleaning
programmes.show_description: F*** Cancer: Shana knows she can be uptight, but talking to friends about a terminal cancer diagnosis is daunting and the Death Cleaners help with her fear of discussing 'the end'.
Stormzy: Stormzy welcomes Louis into his home to discuss his childhood, the breakdown of a high-profile relationship, and the pressures that come with being a musician and role model.
Shopping from Home.
Dave and Olive: Pete meets a puppy who needs urgent treatment for a badly broken leg and a young Doberman who has spent most of her life in quarantine kennels.

Mel, Murphy and Twist: It's Pete's first day at Dogs Trust, and he meets a pug facing a worrying surgery and pregnant dog who was seized at the border.
The Potato Pups: Pete meets a litter of puppies known as the Potato Pups, who were found abandoned in a crisp box, and helps the staff fight to keep them alive.
Dave and Olive: Pete meets a puppy who needs urgent treatment for a badly broken leg and a young Doberman who has spent most of her life in quarantine kennels.
Colm, Ruby, Tammy... and Mel: Pete travels to Northern Ireland to pick up three stray dogs and bring them back to Essex. He also meets a Shar Pei who is in constant pain and may need surgery.
Theo and the Suitcase Pups: Pete returns to Dogs Trust, facing a suitcase of abandoned pups on day one and falling hard for Theo, a gentle Italian Mastiff with a serious heart condition.

Terri, Esme and the Chi Family: Pete meets Lurcher Terri, who's terrified of the world and must learn to trust again, and helps settle a family of chihuahuas given up by their heartbroken owner. S2 Ep
Hugo and The Ice Cream Pups: Pete meets some newborn pups fighting to survive, and helps teach Hugo, a Cane Corso rescued from quarantine at Dover, how to be a dog.
Queen Bee, Winter and Elliot: Pete helps a withdrawn pregnant Malinois regain strength so she can care for her 11 lively pups, and falls for Winter, a Staffie who is covered in scars.
Flower, Heidi and Marvin: Pete meets Flower, the first dog ever to take a dislike to him - can he win her trust? He also helps Heidi and Marvin, both facing serious medical challenges.
Oz, Ollie & Bayleigh: Labrador puppy Ollie is rushed in after being hit by a car, while Bullmastiff Bayleigh has a painful lump near her ankle and adventurous cat Oz's leg needs saving.
The crews are kept busy attending a man with a swollen leg, a fisherman having a stroke and a teenager whose chest pains turn out to be something totally unexpected.
A truck driver is trapped in his crushed cab after a motorway smash, while in Hull, firefighters handcuff a man to the rails of the Humber Bridge to stop him from jumping.
A woman fights for her life after being knifed by her former partner, while a teenager on her way to seeing two YouTube stars in a live appearance is felled by a falling tree.
First-ever episode of the much-loved sitcom that launched Smithy onto the scene! After being phone buddies for months, Gavin and Stacey arrange a rendezvous in London.
When the couple have their first lovers' tiff, Gavin is so beside himself with worry that he drives all the way to Barry Island to make amends.
The couple announce their engagement and the families unite for a party in Billericay, but they are unprepared for a bombshell that awaits them.

programmes.show_name: The Gentle Art of Swedish Death Cleaning
programmes.show_description: The Mother Load: The Death Cleaners help Lindsey after her mother and extended family leave everything they don't want at her house - causing her to lose her personal style.
Dame Judi Dench: Louis meets the beloved Oscar-winning actress for a candid and lively conversation about her long career, her passion for theatre, and life away from acting.
Shopping from Home.
Feet of Clay: Part 3: Heaton's suspect provides an alibi for one of the murders, and helps the police by identifying everyone involved in staging the rave events attended by the victims.
One Year On: When a cab driver is badly beaten and robbed, an electronic travel card found near the crime scene provides a lead to a volunteer working on the Jasmine Allen estate.
Bleeding Hearts: Nate falls for a paramedic called to attend to the victim of a fight, but is later forced to arrest her brother for his part in the incident.
Broken Hearts: A stabbing victim proves uncooperative and clearly has something to hide. Nate is furious to learn his new love interest agreed not to report the crime.
Righteous Kill: Part 1: Max's Polish ancestry proves useful when he investigates corruption in Sun Hill's immigrant community.

The fast-paced reality series continues. HART staff must treat one of their own after a freak accident involving a 4x4 leaves an off-duty medic with serious injuries.
One more shift with Yorkshire Ambulance Service's Hazardous Area Response Team. The team are called into action when an explosion demolishes a block of flats.
The response team race to the rescue of a worker trapped in a collapsed trench, and paramedic Kim must risk her own life to give him medical treatment 10ft underground.
A truck driver is trapped in his crushed cab after a motorway smash, while in Hull, firefighters handcuff a man to the rails of the Humber Bridge to stop him from jumping.
Caroline treats a father of four with severe pancreatitis, Rhona restores her patient's health with a large blood transfusion, and Sarah cares for a mum with incurable stomach cancer.
James and Matron Steph have an influx of admissions in the emergency department, Matron Hazera tries to discharge patients to free up beds, and Katie stays late for a high-risk delivery.
